title: Chemist
description: Apply chemical knowledge to research, analyse substances, and develop products — in industries from pharmaceuticals to petrochemicals, food technology, and materials science.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — needed in manufacturing, mining, and pharmaceutical sectors.
employability.outlook: Stable; materials science and green chemistry growing.
pathways: Pharmaceutical companies, petrochemicals, food industry, research councils

title: Biologist / Life Scientist
description: Study living organisms — from cellular and molecular biology to ecology and zoology. Work in research institutions, conservation agencies, healthcare, agriculture, and biotechnology.

employability.demand: Moderate — academic and government research strongest; private-sector biotech growing.
employability.outlook: Growing; biosecurity, conservation, and biotechnology expanding.
pathways: Universities, CSIR, NBI, conservation organisations, pharmaceutical companies

title: Physicist
description: Study matter, energy, and the fundamental laws of the universe. Physicists work in research, engineering, medical physics, renewable energy, and data-intensive fields.

employability.demand: Moderate — strong in nuclear energy, medical physics, and academic research.
employability.outlook: Growing; renewables, Necsa, ITER project, and data science value physics graduates.
pathways: CSIR, Necsa, universities, renewable energy companies, financial modelling

title: Environmental Scientist
description: Study and manage the environment — assessing pollution, conducting environmental impact assessments, advising on policy, and developing conservation strategies.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — EIA legislation requires environmental practitioners on all major projects.
employability.outlook: Growing; climate change policy and sustainability reporting driving demand.
pathways: EIA consultancies, government, mining companies, conservation agencies

title: Geologist / Geoscientist
description: Study the Earth's structure, materials, and history to locate mineral deposits, assess geological hazards, and support mining, civil engineering, and environmental projects.

employability.demand: High — SA mining sector creates strong demand for exploration and mine geologists.
employability.outlook: Strong; minerals critical to green energy (lithium, manganese, cobalt) driving exploration.
pathways: Mining houses, consulting, Department of Mineral Resources

title: Microbiologist / Biochemist
description: Study microorganisms, their interactions, and biochemical processes — critical in healthcare (infection control, vaccine development), food safety, agriculture, and industrial biotechnology.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — food industry, pharmaceutical, and public health driving demand.
employability.outlook: Growing; pandemic awareness elevated microbiological expertise in policy.
pathways: Pharmaceutical companies, food labs, hospitals, NICD, CSIR

title: Statistician
description: Design experiments, collect data, and apply statistical methods to draw conclusions — supporting research, business decisions, public policy, and scientific study.

employability.demand: High — demand spans science, finance, healthcare, government, and tech.
employability.outlook: Strong; data revolution has elevated the value of statistical skills.
pathways: BSc Statistics / Actuarial Science / Mathematics (3 years)
Stats SA, SARB, insurance, banks, pharmaceutical firms, research councils

title: Mathematician
description: Apply abstract reasoning and mathematical models to solve problems — in pure research, finance, engineering, cryptography, and machine learning. Postgraduate study is typically required.

employability.demand: Moderate — core academic demand plus high crossover to finance and tech.
employability.outlook: Stable; mathematical modelling skills increasingly sought outside academia.
pathways: Universities, financial services, engineering firms, tech companies

title: Oceanographer / Marine Scientist
description: Study the ocean — its physical, chemical, biological, and geological properties. Contribute to fisheries management, climate science, environmental monitoring, and offshore industry.

employability.demand: Moderate — niche but growing given blue economy and climate focus.
employability.outlook: Steady; government science agencies and DFFE are primary employers.
pathways: DFFE, CSIR, South African Weather Service, offshore mining companies
